properties.name: Mletačka utvrda
properties.description: French-style defensive fortress built by the Venetians in the 1600s, with views of the city & bay.
reviews: Amazing views from the top - panoramic of the whole site - was helpful as my first look of the city to get my bearings. Loved that the ticket also included use of the undercity tunnel network - helpful as it was raining on and off (visited a couple of days ago!)
Very beautiful views. Nice fortress, with a tower from where the views are even more wonderful. The entrance fee is 7 euros.

I like history and I've been to quite a few other fortresses and the design follows other fortress designs because you're trying to defend and required similar designs and systems. The fortress has been disarmed and the museums/displays on site are bright with information and displays but I found some lack.....that is not a bad comment, just a minor con. There has obviously been a lot of work to maintain the integrity of the stone work and that is definitely a pro. Definitely worth the time for a visit. I rate this 4 stars.

properties.name: Fort Valmarin
properties.description: Large 19th-century hilltop fortress above Pula, closed to visitors and surrounded by a defensive ditch.
reviews: Closed area. Ministry of Defence. Located 350 meters from the construction material warehouse and store on Labinska Street...

properties.name: Fort Bourguignon
properties.description: 19th-century coastal fortress in Pula, known for its circular design, military history, and cultural events.
reviews: Beautiful Austro-Hungarian fortress which is preserved, but abandoned. In my opinion the most beautiful one in Pula. Unfortunately it is closed for visitors. I recommend to visit in early morning (between 6:00-7:00 AM) because there are not any people around and sunlight is perfect for photography.

Very nice place to visit. Not crowdy. 10 minutes slow walk from free parking space on front of resort ( on right side from the main gate). To reach te place we used wide route on right side of resort entrance on the bushes.

properties.name: Utvrda Marie Louise
properties.description: Large 19th-century coastal fortress on the Muzil peninsula in Pula, known for its circular design and strategic views over the bay entrance.
reviews: The remains of a large defensive fort on Musil peninsula. It was built in the 19th century and used by different armies throughout history. Today it is almost forgotten but still far from a mere ruin. The strength of its walls and beauty of its architecture are still evident. It is safe to explore, yet watch your step for scrap metal, holes and other obstacles. Just like Verudela this ruin should be restored and put to good use.
Spectacular abandoned fort. You can explore everything. Be careful.

There's a good, convenient entrance, but it's guarded. Closed to the curious. However, you can access the area along the western coast along a narrow path. Along the way, you'll encounter abandoned former military fortifications. It's a bit scary! Once inside, I felt like a stalker! I saw several huge peacocks. This was once a huge military base. Now everything is abandoned, but the buildings, both above and below ground, are in excellent condition.

properties.name: Fort Punta Christo
properties.description: Circular fort built in the late 1800s with underground rooms, also a venue for music festivals.

properties.name: Fort Monte Munide
properties.description: Small 19th-century circular artillery tower above Štinjan, surrounded by forest and known for its military history and quiet hilltop setting.
reviews: One of the 19th century artillery forts around Pula. The remains of it to be exact as it was bombarded in WW II and never reconstructed or conserved since. There is a worn-out info panel and a narrow path to the inside of the fort which is overgrown in trees and bush. There is nothing special to see, only to witness the utmost negligence and incompetence of the locals towards their own legacy. Dissapointing.
Nice example of historical forts unfortunately blown up durning ww2

Whole hill is full of military tunnels and bunkers. At the top is historycal Austrohungarian 19. century fort, 50 meters away is entrance to Yugoslavian era bunker, and right bellow the hill towards sea is another large bunker

properties.name: Camp Brioni
properties.description: Seaside campsite near Pula set in a pine forest, with a long pebble beach, views of the Brijuni Islands, and a wide range of sports and accommodation options.
reviews: Camp neat and clean. Sanitary facilities are regularly cleaned and toilet paper is never lacking. Staff friendly. The downside is that the WI FI doesn't work, nor does the data network. Not for Telemach users or Telekom users. It's like there's a dead pocket for the signal there. Pizzeria RR is excellent, as is the Mistral bar, where they have top-notch burgers. The downside of these two bars is that payment is only possible with cash and not with a card.
We rented a mobile home and had a wonderful 12 days. We would gladly do it again.

Toilets were super clean! Very friendly welcome. Quite large, but you don't notice that because of the different sections on the grounds. Just outside the campsite is a very long, well-maintained beach with beach bars that seems to go on forever. A bike or moped is recommended, or I think you can also take the bus to explore the surrounding area. The pebble beach in section 'A' does have a lot of large boulders, but just outside the campsite (past the small harbor) is also pleasant. €95 for 2 nights for 2 people with a dog, electricity on the second row with a sea view.

properties.name: Bizantinski kastrum
properties.description: Layered archaeological site on Veliki Brijun with remains of a late antique settlement, Byzantine-period fortifications, and centuries of continuous life within Brijuni National Park.
reviews: This is definitely one of the most interesting attractions on the island. The conservation of some of the stones and structures is very impressive and gives an idea of what life and work was on those times. These are the remains of an ancient settlement from the 4th century, inhabited even during Venetian rule.
An impressive archeological site. Read the info-panel and walk around, try to imagine the life and architecture of this settlement.
Absolutely recommendable for archaeologically interested visitors. There is still so much to see and explore. One of the best archaeological sites on the island.
If you're cycling around the island, would definitely recommend stopping here on the way around - the size and scale of the preserved castle foundations are quite staggering

properties.name: Safari park
properties.description: Family-friendly safari park on Veliki Brijun with exotic and native animals, an ethno park, and a scenic train ride within Brijuni National Park.
reviews: Brijuni Safari Park is one of the most interesting attractions on the island of Veliki (Great) Brijun. Visitors mostly explore the park through a ride on a train and they can see various animals including zebras, ostriches, llamas, and peacocks. There are also sacred Indian cows (zebu), sea turtles and goats. However, the most impressive animal here is the Asian elephant Lanka who arrived on the island in 1972 as a gift from Indian Prime Minister Indira Gandhi.

In my next life, I want to be born as an animal on Brijuni. The place is so beautiful, with the view on the sea. The grass is green, the animals all seem healthy and happy! Lenka (the elephant) is one of the longest living elephants (she's in her 60s).

I recently had an unforgettable safari experience at the Brijuni Islands in Croatia. The natural beauty of the park is breathtaking, with lush landscapes, diverse wildlife, and stunning views all around. The safari tour was well-organized, and we had the chance to see various animals up close, making it an incredible adventure for nature lovers. One important tip: the ferry ride to the island costs only €10, so make sure you're not overcharged. Be cautious of potential scams, especially from people trying to sell overpriced tickets or unnecessary services outside the official vendors. Other than that, it was a fantastic trip, and I highly recommend it to anyone visiting Croatia!
We really liked our stroll around Brijuni island. The safari park is a nice addition. Definitely check the tickets for the ferry to the island in advance. They sell out fast.

properties.name: Valerijin park
properties.description: Historic triangular park below the Pula Arena, known for its landscaped greenery, Mediterranean plants, and long connection to the city’s urban heritage.
reviews: This is a park located between two major attractions of Pula. There are a playground, some statues, benches to sit down, trees that provide shades, etc.

properties.name: Arena Pula
properties.description: Roman amphitheatre from the 1st century and one of Pula’s most iconic landmarks, known for its monumental architecture, rich history, and major cultural events.
reviews: Incredibly well preserved ruins in a very cool part of town. So much to see around and inside. They also use the middle for various things. They had an ice rink there when I went.

Pula is a small town, so seeing such a grandiose structure from the era of the great Roman Empire here is both unexpected and impressive. There are far fewer visitors than at the Roman Colosseum, so everyone has the unique opportunity to find a secluded spot, stand, and reflect on eternity, on how times, customs, and games have changed over the centuries. Don't miss the small but fascinating museum located in the arena's underground chambers—it's dedicated primarily to olive oil production in Istria.
Gorgeous arena. A must see when in Pula. Beautiful weather helped to get some beautiful shots. More than 2000 years old and one of six biggest amphitheatres still standing.. breathtakingly stunning.

What a site to see! Amazing roman arena built between 27–68 AD. Sixth largest in the world. There is a fee to enter and you are free to walk around once inside. There are QR codes that provide a self guided tour using your phone. Bring ear buds or headphones for the audio. This is one of the best preserved amphitheaters in the world. This is a must see.

properties.name: Fort San Giorgio
properties.description: 19th-century circular hilltop fort in Pula, built as part of the Austro-Hungarian defence system and known for its strong military character.
reviews: A beautiful fort which is worth a 5 star rating! HOWEVER, completely ruined by graffiti and rubbish, see pictures. Government please preserve this amazing object
Nice view, a special scent from the plants when entering, but a little sad that Croatia doesn't take more care of this place that is part of their heritage.

properties.name: Augustov Hram
properties.description: Historic Roman temple on Pula’s Forum, admired for its classical architecture, monumental columns, and lasting connection to the city’s ancient past.
reviews: A remarkably well-preserved gem of Roman architecture, built during the reign of Emperor Augustus, it stands as a graceful reminder of the city’s ancient past. Its elegant columns, finely carved details, and harmonious proportions make it one of the best surviving examples of a Roman temple outside Italy. Inside, the temple houses a small collection of Roman sculptures.

For a couple euro you get to turn back the clock to the time of Christ and envision what the Roman Empire resembled from an architectural perspective and from the statues of that period. Nice little stop in old town Pula.
Loved the temple. Many interesting and beautiful pieces. Good explanation of tenples history. Also, it is a very welcoming stuff that could tell more if you are interested.
The ancient building was bombed during WWII and has been restored with a lot of effort, hard work, and love. 2€ to get in and while it’s small inside it’s a contribution to maintain..

properties.name: Muzej / galerija - Crkva Srca Isusova i Marijina (Sveta Srca)
properties.description: Former church in Pula’s old town, now a museum-gallery space hosting exhibitions, cultural events, and multimedia programmes.
reviews: A little gem hidden in the old town of Pula. Free, quiet, on two levels, eclectic exhibits, special worksheets for children. Loved it.
Free entry, quiet and calm vibes in an old church with multiple levels of art. Would definitely recommend but don't need to spend more than 20-30 minutes here

Free entry. Currently occupied by a selection of highlights from a main art museum in Zagreb, which is undergoing a seismic retrofit. Beautiful space, nicely presented pieces. We were the only people there which is a shame - it deserves more visibility.

properties.name: Park grada Graza
properties.description: Central city park in Pula, created after World War II and known for its shade, archaeological fragments, and relaxed atmosphere near the Forum.
reviews: I love this little hideaway before the forum. Nice place to sit and think about life while under the shade of a small tree
Peaceful park with ancient fragments all over the place among children’s swings makes for an interesting contrast

Fantastic for kids! A couple of advent houses, a small funfair just right for everyone... for both the little ones and the big ones! Enough for everyone! Just right today, not crowded or too many people... The temperature is pleasant for December 2025!

properties.name: Fort Verudella
properties.description: 19th-century coastal fortress on Punta Verudela, now home to Aquarium Pula and known for its military heritage and sea-facing setting.

properties.name: Greenhill
properties.description: Modern one-bedroom apartment in Pula with a terrace, garden, free parking, and airport transfer, located close to the Arena and city centre.
reviews: Very nicely furnished apartment, has everything you need for a good vacation, very friendly hosts, always at hand for any questions.
The apartment itself was equipped with everything I needed for more stay and more. It was located really close to the city which meant getting to it was no issue.
Very nice host. I can only recommend this place. :) Thank you for everything

properties.name: Arheološki muzej Istre
properties.description: Major archaeological museum in Pula focused on Istria’s heritage, with collections and exhibitions across multiple locations while its main building is under renovation.
reviews: Learning something new is amazing. Those tunnels were built long time ago even before my grandpa's time. Just to see that things are not always what seems to be, gives you time to reflect and appreciate what you have now. History is teaching us a good lesson let's don't make the same mistake again. If visit Pula this location gives you a 360 view over the city. The Colosseum is on the East side of the fort.

Pretty great museum if you like history and museums, there's a few rooms on the main level of the citadel. Otherwise, I'd say it's nothing special and the other levels and underground of the citadel are more fascinating.

Hilltop fort in the center of the city. It now houses a museum dedicated, by in large to the occupation of the city, and of Croatia, in second world war. The upper levels provide for excellent view points of the city. It's not expensive to enter and worth a visit during your time in Pula.

properties.name: Amfiteatar
properties.description: Boutique hotel in central Pula, just steps from the Arena, offering modern rooms, a well-known restaurant, and easy access to the old town and waterfront.
reviews: Fantastic hotel, great location, very central. Lovely staff and extremely helpful with suggestions to eat and drink and booking a taxi to the airport. Room 101 was great, really spacious with bathrobes and slippers. Tea and coffee making facilities in the room. Great choice of both hot and cold food at breakfast. Would definitely stay again.
I can’t get over what a great place this is! Location of course, front desk service, comfort, and value. I would highly recommend this facility for any type of visit to Pula.

Great experience staying here! Location is superb and the staff is super friendly. We accidentally broke the tap in the bathroom while staying here, but the hotel staff just exchanged it during the day and it was fixed when we got back in the afternoon. Would definitely recommend staying here!

properties.name: Park Franje Josipa I.
properties.description: Historic waterfront park in Pula, known for its shaded paths, palm trees, family-friendly atmosphere, and relaxed setting near the Riva.
reviews: A beautifully situated park right next to the port of Pula. Thanks to the tall trees, the playground remains shaded even in summer until after midday. A constant sea breeze makes it pleasantly cool despite the high temperatures. Highly recommended. Paid parking is available nearby.
A good place to relax with beautiful views and palm trees all around. There are playgrounds and are close to many tourist attractions in Pula. I recommend it for those with children.
A wonderful park by the harbor in Pula. With plenty of places to relax and a playground for the little ones. Beautifully landscaped and clean.

properties.name: Park Monte Zaro
properties.description: Largest historic park in Pula, set on a hill around the observatory, with fountains, Mediterranean trees, and scenic views over the city and harbour.
reviews: I didn’t expect much, but Montezaro Park turned out to be such a nice surprise. It’s quiet, full and perfect when you need a break from the heat and crowds. I grabbed a coffee and just walked around for a bit…. really relaxing spot with a great local feel.

The roots of Montezaro Park are deeply embedded in Pula’s past. Originally designed in the 19th century, it was a retreat for nobility and a symbol of prestige. Small park that has a fountain, many trails to get around, and plenty of flora areas to enjoy. It's on a small hill so you can get some views of the surrounding area.
A quiet city park on a hill overlooking Pula harbour, a welcome escape far from the madding crowd.

properties.name: Titov park
properties.description: Central waterfront park in Pula, known for its memorial monuments, shaded walking areas, city model, and lively social atmosphere with seasonal events.
reviews: Tito's Park in Pula is a prominent public park that serves as a green oasis in the heart of the city. Named after Josip Broz Tito, the former Yugoslav leader, the park is a popular area for both locals and tourists seeking relaxation and recreation. There's also a miniature model on one side of the park.
Nice park next to the water with many busts of famous partisans, including Tito. The main monument is also dedicated to the partisans. Worth a few pictures.
A piece of more recent Croatian history. Has some statues, a place for kids to play, a few benches and a model of the city.

properties.name: Park kralja Petra Krešimira IV
properties.description: Central city park in Pula with a fountain, Roman mausoleum remains, flower beds, and a peaceful atmosphere close to the waterfront.
reviews: A very nice, well-kept, well-maintained little place in the heart of the city. If you go here, you can sit on one of the small benches and breathe some fresh air while looking at the trees and flowers.

A beautiful little park near Pula's historic center. The fountain in the center, made from a strange rock, is unique and remarkable. Otherwise, this green space is very well-maintained and has a good number of benches for a peaceful rest in the shade.

properties.name: Utvrda Monvidal
properties.description: Historic hilltop fort in Pula with a distinctive horseshoe layout, surrounded by greenery and preserving a raw reminder of the city’s military past.
reviews: Worth checking out but nothing special

The fortress is in very poor condition. It's completely overgrown with trees and grass; you can walk around it in five minutes. There are many interesting stories about it online, but in reality, there's nothing to see. It's a shame such a place is falling into disrepair.
We discovered this fortress by accident during a night walk. Very few people know about it. Despite being neglected, it is a piece of history and worth seeing.

properties.name: Scaletta
properties.description: Quaint hotel in central Pula near the Arena, offering comfortable rooms, breakfast, parking, and easy access to the city’s historic sights.
reviews: Small hotel ,central -5' from the amphitheater ,10' from the historic center of the pleasant town of Pula to walk along the tree-lined Rambla between arches small squares churches from the soaring bell towers to the port.Like the city - bright clean and cozy -so the small hotel in retro style ', slightly French proves safe and fulfilling. It is true that the external stairs lead to the familiar reception and that breakfast is served on the room overlooking the street, -I think a former very chic restaurant whose dishes and crockery now serve an essential breakfast - but the pleasure is given by the central heart just a stone's throw from the Colosseum, magnificent!

We cannot thank the lovely owners enough for all their help with finding a car for us yesterday. The hotel is a real find and value for money and close to the ferry and the city centre. Lovely breakfast also.

properties.name: Park Mladenaca
properties.description: Small central park in Pula near the Forum, known for its landscaped greenery, evening atmosphere, and popularity for wedding photos and sunset walks.
reviews: A great place to watch sunset.
A small park next to the ancient temple.

A small green space near the central Roman Forum and the Temple of Augustus. It's certainly not the best place for jogging or long walks, but it's very well-kept, both for its lawn and trees, and boasts a curious monument located within. It's certainly a great place for a relaxing break in the shade, surrounded by stunning monuments.

properties.name: Hostel Joyce
properties.description: Boutique hostel in the heart of Pula, set beside the Arch of the Sergii and known for its modern rooms, historic character, and walkable location.
reviews: Wonderful hotel with modern rooms, comfortable beds, spectacular views and an unbeatable location. Staff was warm and inviting and had everything prepared for us. Check in and check out was a breeze! Much more affordable than other 4- star hotels in comparable tourist-centric cities (especially during seasonal rush). Will definitely return.
I strongly recommend this hotel: my room was large and clean, Nikola, the boy at the reception desk, was very helpful. Furthermore, guests have a 20 % discount at the adjacent restaurant!

Despite the room being quite small, it was ideal for an overnight stay, was reasonably priced and in a fantastic location. The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful. The room was very clean, quiet and comfortable (the AC was very welcome!). It's in the perfect location to see all the main sights and is also a short walk from restaurants, bars and a bus stop. Thanks for a lovely stay!

I would describe the hotel accommodation as practical, picturesque and modern. Our room was quite small but it was absolutely everything we needed. The bed was very comfortable, the air conditioning was very welcome and the view from the window was of the Triumphal Arch. The hotel is in the centre of Pula, so it is a short walk to the sights, good food and the bus stop to the beach or the station. There is also a great restaurant downstairs for brunch. Thank you very much, the stay was wonderful.

properties.name: Galerija C8
properties.description: Small contemporary gallery in central Pula, run by the Archaeological Museum of Istria, hosting archaeological, artistic, and educational exhibitions.

properties.name: Fort Casoni Vecchi
properties.description: Historic circular hilltop fort in Pula, known for its Austro-Hungarian military heritage, panoramic views, and occasional cultural events.
reviews: The Fort was built in 1852-1854 during the wars; currently stands empty and locked off to visitors. Nothing to see here, overgrown and rather dangerous.

Beautiful structure that unfortunately is abandoned/locked from tourists. The Fort is hidden behind a big building and walls which hides its former beauty. It is in bad shape. Nothing to see here unfortunately.

properties.name: Hotel Pula
properties.description: Large hotel near the beach in Pula, offering comfortable rooms, sports and wellness facilities, and easy access to both the coast and the city’s historic sights.
reviews: It’s hard to choose a hotel based on the reviews, as everyone has an expectation. This hotel is located near the beach, in the middle of a beautiful landscape. Offers parking, wi-fi, gym, swimming pool, breakfast and others. The quaro, though old, was clean and cozy. The service from the staff was excellent, as I had made a reservation that was cancelled and I was surprised at the time, but the boy, very kind, solved the problem. Thank you very much for your attention. The price was a little above the platform, but it was already a little late and we ended up staying there.

This place really needs some love. Sure, lots of options for breakfast, but the visual aspect of spending time here leaves much to be desired. Ancient caprets in the halls tell stories. Not ones I would like to be part of.

It's an old hotel that must have been great in its day. Now it's worn out but makes no pretensions about itself. It has got a nice looking pool but I didn't try it. The rooms are basic and the bathroom is best described as old but functional. Generally the hotel is clean and the staff friendly.

properties.name: Mornarički Park
properties.description: Historic 19th-century park in Pula and the city’s oldest public park, known for its botanical richness, shaded paths, and children’s play areas.
reviews: Very nice park with fountains and children playground. Quiet place near main street, ideal for relax during hiking around Pula.
Great place for stop and chill during walking through Pula.

properties.name: MONTE ZARO
properties.description: Modern hostel in Pula with private bathrooms, breakfast, free parking, and a convenient location close to city attractions.
reviews: We were here in August 2025 And it was perfect, receptionists were always nice and helpfull, the breakfast was delicious, the room was clean, new and quite nice. The location is perfect, we were 10 minutes away from the city center. So basically, i recommend this hostel to anyone, who don’t want to Pay big cash, but do want to spend time in comfort accommodation.
Nice apartment for one night stay. Walking distance from city centre, Arena. Useful kitchen. Good value for money.
Perfect location if you want to explore the city of Pula by foot. The room was clean and the staff was very helpful. Walls are quite thin and you can hear other people in the hall.

properties.name: Villa Carla
properties.description: Spacious villa apartment in Pula near Verudela, offering sea views, a shared pool, parking, and easy access to beaches, restaurants, and city attractions.
reviews: Robert was fantastic. He was extremely welcoming during our stay and helped us with anything we needed. The place was great and easily big enough for the 7 of us. 100% would come back. Thanks Robert

Robert welcomed us on arrival and gave us a tour of the apartment. Such a nice and friendly soul. Very helpful and thoughtful and we made plans to speak to Robert during our stay so he could advise us of places to go and trips to do. Our first evening we spent at a restaurant just a few yards away and his recommendation was spot on. We returned there several times during our stay (Sopranos). Our stay was fantastic - the pool area is relatively small and is sometimes shaded but is a lovely spot. The front of the accommodation has a purpose built bbq area (covered) that we also made good use of a couple of evenings. The local grocery store is a two minute walk away. Go to the butchers section - you’ll be amazed at the quality and price of the fresh meat on offer. A 15 minute walk got us into Pula itself - the nearest beach is 10 minutes away and we were able to have a boat trip a day out in Rovinj and threw some fun on inflatables and quad bikes for good measure. A great stay.

Truly exceptional hosts. So friendly and helpful, we were made to feel like family. The apartment had everything we needed and was a good base location for the things we wanted to explore. Hope to visit again one day!

properties.name: Hostel Art & Joy
properties.description: Friendly hostel in Pula with a garden, terrace, shared kitchen, and convenient access to the Arena and other city attractions.
reviews: We had an amazing stay in this hostel. The staff is very nice and helpful : the receptionist gave us tips on what to see and visit around the city, helped us book bikes. We were also able to change beds to be closer to each other in the room. The bedrooms and common rooms are cleaned daily and the air conditioning was appreciated. The hostel is well located, there are bus stops right next to it, it’s only a 10-minutes walk to the city center. As 2 girls travelling, we felt very confortable and safe staying at this hostel. People we met there were easygoing and we had some really nice times in the kitchen and on the terrace. To sum up : we had a wonderful vacation in Pula :) PS : the only thing that’s missing would be board games accessible to everyone.

We had a nice stay in the private room. Everything is clean, the staff is friendly, pizzeria next door. The cute mosaique and artistic flair got us lost in long conversations about art and the meaning of life ;)

I have stayed in Pula 5 times now. The first time was at an apartment with restricted check in times. 3pm to 5pm only. And a long walk to the centre.I was in a restaurant chatting to someone on the next table.He recommended Art and Joy hostel. I took a walk there the next day. His advice was spot on. I met the manager there and said may I have a look inside, with a possibility of staying there the following year. She agreed. Wow it was great I thought. So I booked for the next Summer. I wasn't disappointed. I have now stayed 4 times each Summer for 4 years.Close to the centre, and the staff are superb. They will help you with any issues you may have.. The hard working cleaner is great also. I recommend it. Choice of rooms to suit your requirements.And good value.. Give it a try for a few nights and then extend it if you want to. Obviously subject to availability.

properties.name: Huguesov park
properties.description: City park in Pula known for its greenery, quiet central setting, and a bust of Nikola Tesla that gives the space memorial significance.
reviews: A lovely little park with a monument to Nikola Tesla.

Great benches and perfect place to come with a book. Best during sunny days as there is a lot of shade and the breeze is nice. Highly recommend for locals or if you are visiting nice break/chill area outside as this area isn’t crowded.

properties.name: Apartmani Pula Arena
properties.description: Modern apartments in central Pula near the Arena, offering equipped kitchens, free WiFi, and easy access to the city’s main sights.
reviews: The apartment is perfectly located (right next to the Arena of Pula) to explore the city. Also it is very close to the ferry terminal which is convenient if you travel onwards with the ship.
The apartment is in an excellent location, in a dilapidated building. The interior furnishings are okay, but there's room for improvement.

Very good location, unfortunately a forest in front obscures the view of the sea. You have to see it from the upper windows. Clean and tidy. The second level of the apartment is like a loft with beds. Quiet and pleasant.

properties.name: SWEET APARTMENT TONY
properties.description: Comfortable apartment in Pula with a terrace, balcony, free private parking, and a fully equipped kitchen, set a short drive from the Arena.
reviews: Family was help-full and kind. Even gave us a homemade cake :) We had all we needs in their apartment. Close to the apartment is shopping mall with all variety of shops. 3 min by car, maybe 10 by foot.

Everything was perfect! The owner was super helpful and friendly! They helped us with bringing the suit see and provided everything we needed. The apartment is super well equipped and kids friendly. Supermarket, city center and beaches are nearby. The best part was AC in this hot summer! Sofa bed could be better but manageable
Very nice apartment with big bathroom, well equipped kitchen, air conditioning in each room, large terrace.

properties.name: Apartments Villa Bubi
properties.description: Comfortable apartments near the beach in Pula, offering terraces, free parking, barbecue facilities, and easy access to the city and Brijuni.
reviews: Great helpful staff, nice and welcoming place. Close to the coast (5-10 minutes on foot), with great beaches. Guarded parking, air conditioning, garden grill. I recommend it to everyone!
If you’re looking for a nice and quiet place to stay, with beautiful garden, this is place for you. Clean, cozy, pet friendly.. great host.. there is a parking lot, bikes to rent..

This apartment is at a beautiful location, near to the beach, with nice and clean, well-equipped rooms (air conditioner, balcony). The host is very friendly and helpful. I would totally recommend to book a room in Villa Bubi! :)

properties.name: Hotel Park Plaza Arena Pula
properties.description: Modern seaside hotel on Punta Verudela in Pula, offering sea views, saltwater pools, wellness facilities, and direct access to the beach in a pine forest setting.
reviews: Lovely hotel, with an excellent buffet breakfast, comfortable rooms, efficient laundry service, and a short walk to the sea, with a beautiful view; we would stay there again

So far, it's been a fantastic holiday. The rooms are quite modern and perfectly adequate for two people. There's a wide selection at breakfast, and the food is very tasty. The staff is always friendly and helpful. We recommend booking bed and breakfast, as there are plenty of restaurants on this small peninsula. What bothers us personally is that no one seems to be monitoring the hotel's sunbathing area. About 50% of the people who lounge there aren't hotel guests, so it's impossible to find a spot by lunchtime. You're basically forced to reserve sunbeds early in the morning.
What a beautiful location! The beds were very comfortable. The breakfast was amazing!

properties.name: Arena Stoja
properties.description: Seaside campsite on the Stoja peninsula in Pula, offering sea-view pitches, a peaceful natural setting, and direct access to the rocky coast.
reviews: Beautiful campsite on the Adriatic coast. We watched the dolphins in the evenings, which was very impressive. There's a very good restaurant on site, and everything is very clean. The staff at reception are very friendly, and the sanitary facilities are constantly cleaned. The bus to Pula stops right in front of the campsite, and you can also easily walk to the Colosseum in 35 minutes. We really enjoyed our stay here and would love to come back. The only minor drawback is that, unlike most campsites, there are no dog waste bags available anywhere on the grounds. A dog costs €7 per day.

During our Istria tour in April 2026, we stayed here for two nights in our campervan. Five stars are well-deserved. Everything was clean and very nice. We were parked in the first row right by the sea and enjoyed the sunset. It was very quiet at night, and the staff were very friendly. We would gladly come back.
Beautiful campsite by the sea. Clean and excellent sanitary facilities. Friendly welcome. Many facilities. Good price.

properties.name: Park žrtava fašizma
properties.description: Neighbourhood park in Šijana, Pula, with a children’s playground, benches, and a pleasant green space for everyday relaxation.

properties.name: Fort Turtian
properties.description: Historic polygonal fortress near Pula, built as part of the city’s defensive ring and still standing prominently above the surrounding landscape.
reviews: A beautiful  Austro-Hungarian fortress with a beautiful sunset over Pula.

We were there at the end of September. The only visitors to the fort were a few goats. The gate was closed to everyone else. We walked all the way around, and there's definitely no access. There's a sign at the entrance stating that the army abandoned the fort in 1992, and after that, it gradually fell into disrepair due to vandalism. Now, some local people have come together to clear out the fort, and perhaps there will be an opportunity to visit it on special days. There were no official opening hours listed. So, based on current information, it's not worth the trip.

properties.name: Park pod Arenom
properties.description: Historic landscaped park beneath the Pula Arena, known for its flower beds, elegant Italian-era design, and one of the city’s most photogenic settings.

properties.name: Slavoluk Sergijevaca
properties.description: Historic Roman arch in central Pula, known as the Arch of the Sergii or Golden Gate, famous for its ornate reliefs and long-standing civic significance.
reviews: The Golden Gate in Pula is a beautifully preserved historic landmark. Its Roman architecture and detailed stonework make it a must-see in the city center. A great spot for a quick walk, photos, and soaking in the ancient atmosphere.

A Roman triumphal arch, beautifully intact through the ages. Its intricate carvings reflect the artistry of the Augustan era, while its enduring form stands as a testament to the power and legacy of ancient Rome.
Beautiful Roman arch in the heart of Pula. The Arch of the Sergii is a stunning reminder of the city's ancient past. Great spot for photos and easy to find while walking through the old town.

Another beautiful piece of history which must be seen to be appreciated when you’re in Pula. Seems to be the main entrance essentially to the late ish night shops and restaurants/bars etc. Did have a nice jazz band situated here one night too!

properties.name: Dvojna vrata
properties.description: Ancient Roman city gate in Pula with two arched passages, notable stone decoration, and direct links to some of the city’s key archaeological sites.
reviews: Nice looking arch together with the walls in the heart of Pula. Reminds me like I'm in Italy. However, it would just need to be cleaned from the black patina that has accumulated on it over time so it can look even nicer.
Indeed, the two gates look identical. The fact that they are about a thousand years old is fascinating.
A pair of twelfe entrances in ancient roman and mediaval Pula...but if you want to know more - hire a local licenced tour guide...

properties.name: Grand Hotel Brioni Pula
properties.description: Luxury seaside hotel in Pula with panoramic Adriatic views, elegant rooms and suites, fine dining, spa and fitness facilities, and direct access to the beach.
reviews: Fantastic hotel! Fantastic place! Highly recommended if you really want to relax. Even though it's the same chain as the hotel near the Plaza (which I've stayed at three times), I only recommend the Brioni from now on. The Plaza is like a highway motel. I regret not discovering the Brioni sooner. The Plaza should take a cue from its neighbor, from service to comfort.

We were here before Easter, so it was pleasantly quiet. The hotel is very clean and offers a beautiful view, even from the sauna, which is also very well-maintained and inviting. The room service was perfect and couldn't have been better. I'm not sure if the buffet selection is always so limited. For the price, we would have liked a bit more variety, especially at breakfast. However, the quality of the food was consistently very good. Overall, a lovely stay with a few minor drawbacks; we would come back.
I really enjoyed my stay. Everything was as it should be in a hotel, I will definitely recommend this place.
The best hotel we've been to. Great and friendly stuff, helped us with all of our questions, clean rooms and good food

properties.name: Park kralja Zvonimira
properties.description: Peaceful waterfront park in Pula with shaded benches, a children’s playground, and cultural landmarks including the Marulić monument and the Balašević bench.
reviews: A beautiful green space with several seating areas offering stunning views of the sea, shaded by beautiful trees. The lawn is beautiful and well-kept, and the surrounding area is very clean. Definitely a recommended spot for a lovely stroll.

properties.name: Fort Ovine
properties.description: Abandoned Austro-Hungarian coastal fort in the Valovine area, known for its atmospheric tunnels, overgrown walls, and striking rooftop views over the sea.
reviews: It was a small ruin with an entrance, a hallway, some rooms and a tunnel to a small katacomb. The dark tunnel which looks like the entrance of a katacomb has a slight slope and is slippery. Unfortunately some of the rooms were used as toilets - no understanding for that... The parking lots are kind off in front of fort and very busy. So make shure to plan your visit in the morning.
Nothing special about this place, I have only visited the exterior though. I don't know if I suggest the visit. There is a civil guard near the entrance
A fortification overgrown in the rock. Large areas are empty and dark. For those who enjoy extreme sports, this is for you.

properties.name: Park Sergio Endrigo
properties.description: Small urban park in central Pula dedicated to singer-songwriter Sergio Endrigo, with commemorative installations and occasional cultural events. Today it is mostly a quiet stop with a few benches and greenery, more suitable for a short pause than as a main attraction.

properties.name: Park žrtava Vergarole
properties.description: Small memorial park beside Pula Cathedral dedicated to the victims of the Vergarola tragedy of 18 August 1946. More than a classic city park, it is a place of remembrance and reflection, marked by a memorial stone and commemorative ceremonies honoring one of the most painful events in Pula’s modern history.
reviews: Monument to the Pula explosion of 1946. In a well-kept park, in a good environment.

A truly well-maintained green space, both with its immaculate lawn and its many trees. It's certainly not a place you'd think of as a park, but more like a war memorial, commemorated by the plaque at its center, erected in their memory.

properties.name: Art Hostel Pipištrelo
properties.description: Hostel Pipištrelo is a small, stylish hostel in the heart of Pula, just a short walk from the Forum, Arena, Kastel and other main sights of the old town. It offers air-conditioned rooms, free WiFi, a shared lounge and a shared kitchen, combining a central location with a simple, comfortable stay. With only a few rooms and artistic details throughout the interior, the hostel has a more intimate, boutique feel than a classic budget hostel.
reviews: Always ready for help with anything or any need. Maybe a little tomuch aerosols but I air the room before .

Perfect location with a view of the water from our room, 1km from amphitheater, and only minutes to walk to all restaurants and shops. Beaches 10 mins by bus. It was hot during our stay, and communal areas do not have aircon.
Great location walking distance to plenty of bars/restaurants. Clean rooms and very responsive host. Highly recommend!

properties.name: Havajska
properties.description: Tiny pebble beach surrounded by cliffs & accessible by carved staircase, known for its strong waves.
reviews: Excellent free beach near Pula. We went there the last week of August, arriving around 10:00 AM. The main reef was crowded, but there were still some shaded spots available. A good spot to dive into the blue water and relax. Bring your own gear, as it's free and there are no services nearby. The water is clear, with some small fish but also many transparent jellyfish. Free parking is about a 10-minute walk away.

The beach is beautifully located, but unfortunately very crowded. Due to the nearby hotel complexes and the popularity of the place, there are a lot of people on the beach. There are also many jellyfish there. It is purely a rocky and pebble beach, so a sleeping mat or similar may be useful for lying down.

Great beach here, very secluded and has it's own privacy! Beach is rocky of course, but one of the better ones and very clear. Can have a great swim here. Easy to get up and down from the beach via the stairs. Shady areas to the sides.

properties.name: Borcima narodnooslobodilačke borbe i žrtvama fašizma 1941-1945
properties.description: Expressive memorial artwork in Tito’s Park near the Pula waterfront, dedicated to anti-fascist fighters and victims of wartime violence. Its dramatic sculptural composition symbolizes suffering, resistance, remembrance and hope, making it an important commemorative landmark in the city.

properties.name: HI Hostel Pula
properties.description: Beachside hostel in a pine-forest setting near Valsaline, offering simple budget accommodation a short drive from Pula’s old town and Arena. It is a practical choice for solo travellers, groups and school trips, with shared social spaces, a terrace, free WiFi, free public parking and easy access to the sea, Lungomare promenade and outdoor activities.
reviews: I loved everything!! The staff was very lovely and helpful in every way!! Everyone around was very friendly, I definitely plan to come back soon!!, even tho I booked the cheapest room the receptionist made sure I had the most comfortable and safe stay as a solo traveler woman. The beautiful beach is not too far from the city center, you can take a bus and it's only 15min away from the main bus station.

location right at the beach, available free parking, serve breakfast, yet be realistic they match the price i.e. they give cheap bread, butter jam cereals cheap coffee that's it (value 1-2 euros). no kitchen there. nearly no night life. you have to drive or take bike to the center Pula.

I arrived here after a long day of traveling, at dusk, exhausted. Given its location and the lack of many dining options nearby, my dinner consisted of two beers, a bag of chips, and ice cream. The sunset views were incredible. I thought it was a beautiful place. Now for the downsides: it was super noisy. The rooms smelled awful, felt dirty, and the showers were poorly maintained. It's very far from the center. It took me an hour to find it. The breakfast was mediocre. The staff was extremely unfriendly.

properties.name: Fort Tegetthoff
properties.description: Large circular Austro-Hungarian coastal fort on Veliki Brijun, preserved under military control and closed to visitors.

properties.name: Ambrela Beach
properties.description: Popular pebble beach on Verudela in Pula, known for its shallow sea access, family-friendly setting, nearby parking, and a wide range of beach facilities and water activities.
reviews: It was crowded, even though we were here after rain. It gets deep quickly, there were thousands of small jellyfish in the water, it was strange at first, but you get used to it. We were able to park for free at ARTPARK, although it is difficult to find a free space. Nice, clean beach, there is a side beach, showers are free.
Clean water, pebble beach. But very crowded in summer.
A gorgeous beach. The surrounding area is beautiful and clean. There's a couple of meters of sandy entry into the water. Otherwise, there are rocks all around, so protect your feet!
A very nice beach. Crystal-clear water. Not cold. A food and drink stand. Restrooms. Everything you need for a day out.

properties.name: Battery San Giovanni
properties.description: Historic coastal battery above Verudela in Pula, built as part of the Austro-Hungarian defence system and known for its ramparts, military character, and location near Aquarium Pula and the beaches.
reviews: Very beautifully made and presented. This facility houses the second part of the aquarium, which gathers more exotic animal species and provides a look at the development and power of the Austro-Hungarian Navy. I was enchanted by everything I saw in both facilities. You should definitely visit the Pula Aquarium. It is relaxing, inspiring, enchanting. Highly recommended.

The second part of the aquarium is located in the Baterija San Giovanni. Tickets must be purchased at the upper level. This section of the aquarium is dedicated to 'exotic' animal species and concludes with a marine exhibit. The aquariums are well worth seeing. Our children were thrilled. Important on hot days... the aquarium is air-conditioned. The conversion of the fort into an aquarium is also very well done. In short: absolutely worth seeing.
The second part of the aquarium, with the larger animals.

properties.name: Hotel Neptun
properties.description: Historic hotel on Veliki Brijun in Brijuni National Park, known for its spacious rooms, Austro-Hungarian character, peaceful setting, and sea or park views.
reviews: The stay at the hotel was really great. The staff is extremely friendly and always ready to help, and the rooms were clean and tidy. Everything was at a high level and worthy of every compliment. I definitely recommend it!
The staff is very helpful, the rooms are very clean, it's obvious that it's older, especially the bathrooms, but it's clean. The food is average.

Beautiful hotel, rooms are neat and the maids clean without any prompting, with an emphasis on maintaining privacy. The hotel staff is polite and helpful, and commendable for always greeting us when they passed by. We had a room facing the park, so we never had any noise. The food in the restaurant is commendable, as is the rest of the hotel. With accommodation in the hotel, you get discounts on car rentals. Definitely recommended!

properties.name: Hotel Karmen
properties.description: Historic seaside guesthouse on Veliki Brijun, offering simple rooms with park or sea views, old-world charm, and a peaceful stay in Brijuni National Park.
reviews: This island is beautiful and has a sense of peace, even on August 15th! The property still has its 1960s furnishings, but it's clean and charming. The terrace overlooking the sea is truly beautiful and relaxing. It's true that there's no air conditioning or mini-bar, but they provided us with a couple of fans, and we managed just fine. What can I say? The island is so beautiful, it's absolutely worth it.

Wonderful, spacious rooms, just the right amount of nostalgia. In fact, everything is reminiscent of the 60s and 70s and I think it really is from that era, except for the beds, which are new and very comfortable. Again, it's kind of logical that it should stay that way. Everything is very clean. Breakfast is perfect. Staff is very friendly.

Great view from the balcony, 70s flair – anyone who embraces that will enjoy a lovely and relaxing holiday. Clean and the room is cleaned daily. The only drawback is the lack of air conditioning. The buffet food at Hotel Neptun is excellent.

properties.name: Čamčarnica
properties.description: Interactive permanent exhibition in the historic Boats House on Veliki Brijun, presenting the natural and cultural heritage of Brijuni through multimedia, themed rooms, and hands-on displays.
reviews: Definitely something great for kids

properties.name: Rimska vila u uvali Verige
properties.description: Extensive Roman villa complex in Verige Bay on Veliki Brijun, known for its seaside setting, temple remains, mosaics, and importance as one of the finest examples of Roman residential architecture in Istria.

properties.name: Vila Lovorka
properties.description: Elegant seaside villa on Veliki Brijun with sea views towards Valbandon and Pula, offering spacious accommodation, a spa area, a Mediterranean garden, and a peaceful stay enriched by historic stone fragments.

properties.name: Vila Dubravka
properties.description: Elegant seaside villa on Veliki Brijun with sea views, a heated pool, sauna, jacuzzi, and private accommodation surrounded by Mediterranean greenery in Brijuni National Park.

properties.name: Vila Primorka
properties.description: Spacious seaside villa on Veliki Brijun with sea views, retro-inspired interiors, a private rocky beach, and a peaceful setting surrounded by the natural beauty of Brijuni National Park.

properties.name: Brioni
properties.description: Popular beach on the Verudela peninsula in Pula, known for its mix of rocky, concrete, and pebble areas, clear water, family-friendly setting, and wide range of beach facilities and water sports.
reviews: Yes, pleasant memories, wonderful places to visit. I highly recommend it.
The beach is overrun with 'sea nuts' that look like jellyfish (but aren't stinging), making a peaceful swim impossible.
Beautiful spot for a week off.  Enjoyed the watersports and had laughs>

properties.name: Fort Stoja
properties.description: Historic coastal fortress on the Stoja peninsula in Pula, built as part of the Austro-Hungarian defence system and known for its preserved military architecture and strategic position above the southern approach to the harbour.
reviews: Very beautiful places with the opportunity to jump from cliffs.

I unexpectedly discovered this place, a mound. Fort Stoja is one of the most mysterious and 'hidden' forts in Pula. Built in 1884 by the Austro-Hungarians as part of a powerful coastal defense for the empire's main naval base. Unlike most forts, Stoja is almost completely dug into the peninsula's limestone cliff: only a massive entrance wall with a portal is visible from the outside, while the rest is hidden underground and covered by a dense pine forest. It is surrounded by a deep moat cut into the rock, once filled with water and protected by caponiers. On three sides, the fort plunges directly into the sea, making it virtually invisible from the water or from the air. Inside are long casemates, powder magazines, and posterns extending into the hillside. After the First World War, it lost its military significance and gradually became overgrown with forest, becoming a 'ghost fort.' Today the moat is dry, but the walls and entrance are impressive: it seems as if the mountain itself opens a huge door to the 19th century. An ideal place for those who enjoy the atmosphere of abandoned fortifications, peace and quiet, and views of the Adriatic without the crowds of tourists.
Beautiful campsite, convenient for Pula by bus. The fort is not accessible and is at the top of the peninsula.

properties.name: Maketa Pule
properties.description: Interactive tactile model of Pula in the city centre, offering a detailed 3D overview of the city layout, waterways, and key landmarks in a quick and educational way.
reviews: I love seeing the miniature cities; and this one is even cooler because it has water spouting where the Adriatic Sea is; really neat.
Small model of the city of Pula, with water. Good for exploring the city. Made of metal.
A visual miniature of the charming city of Pula. It's a great introduction to general knowledge. I highly recommend it.

properties.name: Park Plaza Histria Pula
properties.description: Premium seaside hotel on Punta Verudela in Pula, offering sea and marina views, indoor and outdoor pools, a large wellness and spa area, and easy access to the beach and city centre.
reviews: Nice hotel in a good location on the outskirts of town. Especially popular with tennis players. Great rooms, upscale amenities. Food is mediocre. Otherwise highly recommended.
Top hotel in Pula. Great location, plenty of things to do and to see

Great place to be close to the water and tennis/pickleball courts. Breakfast was great with many options and the bar was always popular with the bartenders taking time to answer questions and get to know the many different clients and all spoke multiple languages proficiently. Manuela was our favorite suggesting new drinks and asking if we needed anything.

It is situated in one of the most beautiful spots on the Verudela Peninsula. The spa and wellness center is perfect for relaxation, and the massage services are top-notch. With the on-site shopping street and various dining options, you don't even feel the need to leave the hotel. Watching the sunset by the pool in the evening is truly priceless. While there are suitable areas for families with children, the overall atmosphere is calm and prestigious. If you want to visit the historic center of Pula, you can reach it in about 10-15 minutes by public transport or taxi. The only thing that disappointed me was that I booked a room in the 5-star section of the hotel through Booking, but when I arrived, they gave us rooms in the older hotel buildings. Despite being an old building, the rooms had been renovated and were comfortable.

properties.name: Fort Peneda
properties.description: Large Austro-Hungarian coastal fortress on Veliki Brijun, known for its preserved military architecture, strategic position above the southern shore, and continued use as a restricted military site.

properties.name: ACI Marina Pula
properties.description: City marina in the heart of Pula, known for its protected harbour setting, full nautical services, boat rentals, and unique views of the Roman Arena.
reviews: ACI Marina in Pula is a top-notch spot for boaters and visitors alike. Well-maintained docks, friendly staff, and beautiful views make it perfect for sailing, relaxing by the water, or enjoying nearby cafes and restaurants.

Very good Marina restaurant, helpful and friendly Marinero. Clean sanitary facilities, in the middle of the city. Can be noisy sometimes. Marina ist best contacted if you are already quite near if via VHF. Mobile phone is also possible.
The marina is nice and spacious. You can walk above the coast and admire the boats and views.

properties.name: LABIRINT MUDROSTI I ZNANJA
properties.description: Stone labyrinth in Šijanska šuma designed for contemplative walking and a symbolic experience associated with wisdom, learning, clarity, and personal growth.

properties.name: LABIRINT POVEZANOSTI
properties.description: Stone labyrinth in Šijanska šuma intended for reflective walking and a symbolic experience of connection, understanding, and calm in a forest environment.

properties.name: LABIRINT ENERGIJE
properties.description: Stone labyrinth in Šijanska šuma designed for mindful walking, relaxation, and an energy-focused symbolic experience in nature.

properties.name: LABIRINT OSJEĆAJA
properties.description: Stone labyrinth in Šijanska šuma designed for reflective walking and a symbolic experience connected with emotions, sensitivity, calmness, and inner harmony.

properties.name: LABIRINT MOĆI
properties.description: Stone labyrinth in Šijanska šuma designed for reflective walking, relaxation, and a symbolic experience connected with strength, focus, and inner balance.

properties.name: Stara maslina (ulika)
properties.description: Ancient olive tree in Brijuni National Park, estimated to be over 1,600 years old, known as one of the oldest olive trees in the Mediterranean and a remarkable natural and cultural landmark.
reviews: This specific olive tree, which appears to be over 1600 years old, has become one of the main attractions on the largest island of Brijuni and is also one of the most photographed by tourists. It's one of the oldest trees in the Mediterranean.
This is according to knowledge and available information, this is one of the oldest olive trees in the world, a 1600-year-old olive tree.
Really beautiful tree that stands there for cca 1600 years.

properties.name: Agripinina kuća
properties.description: Archaeological site in central Pula near the Roman Forum, known for its layered remains from pre-Roman to early medieval periods and for the discovery of a portrait of Agrippina the Younger.
reviews: Wild looking ruins that are right behind a liquor store. You can walk around the platforms to get better views. There are also 2 cats.
Archaeological site, excavated house from the Roman period. A fun contrast with the modern style. Behind of a busy streets. It is nice and quiet here.
Beautiful remains, 'hidden' between houses which i think gives it charm. There were also a lot of adorable cats

properties.name: Aquarium Pula
properties.description: Aquarium in Fort Verudela and Battery San Giovanni in Pula, known for its Adriatic and exotic marine exhibits, sea turtle rescue centre, and unique setting within historic Austro-Hungarian fortifications.
reviews: We (family of 7) visited during the holiday season. The price was excellent (half price period), there were not many people. We bought the tickets online, in advance. The whole experience was great, totally worth the trip from Rovinj. There are two exhibitions, international and mediterranean. Both were quite enjoyable. The only problem was that we have a dog, who is not allowed inside. It is absolutly understandable (however there are even zoos, where they are welcome: like in Ljubljana Zoo), and the surrounding area is excellent for walks, so we diveded our forces, and went in separately, in two groups. Not ideal, but it was a great decision not to miss the aquarium. Recommended!

We had such a great time here. It was late September and really not too many people. The building (old fortress) ia very impressive and the perfect location for this beautiful Aquarium. It consists of two buildings, old tunnels and areas outside. Our daughter loved it and we did too. 100% recommend

This was a wonderful surprise! I didn’t expect it to be so big and to have so many different species. My 9-month-old loved it and was fascinated by everything. I highly recommend bringing your family here and buying tickets online, as the regular line can get quite long, while the online ticket line was empty.

properties.name: Fratarski
properties.description: Seasonal island campsite on Fratarski otok near Pula, known for its pine forest setting, clean sea, family-friendly beach, and relaxed summer atmosphere focused on nature, camping, and outdoor activities.
reviews: Great camping, somewhat spartan, but also very crowded. It is hard to find a decent spot in the shade.
There should be more camps like this! Just nature, sea and tents! Perfect location :)
Perfect way to enjoy the nature.

properties.name: Zlatićev trg
properties.description: Small landscaped park in Pula with a few benches and a neatly arranged green space, located between roads and suited for a short pause rather than a longer stay.

properties.name: Fort Brioni Minor
properties.description: Large Austro-Hungarian coastal fortress on Mali Brijun, known for its preserved military architecture, strategic sea-facing position, and summer theatre performances held within the fort.
reviews: We have a chance to enjoy there as part of the King Lear play. Fortress is amasing an perfectly fitted the show.
Amazing still standing fortress from the beginning of 20th century. Used by Ulysses theatre
A beautiful place, lots of wonderful things to see, from rich flora and fauna to beautiful historical buildings.

properties.name: Malo rimsko kazalište
properties.description: Historic Roman theatre in the heart of Pula, dating back to the 1st century, today restored as a unique open-air archaeological site and performance venue that combines ancient heritage with contemporary cultural events.
reviews: I attended a Ludovico Einaudi concert at the Small Roman Theatre in Pula and it was amazing. The venue is intimate and historic, giving the performance a magical atmosphere. Perfect for enjoying music up close in a unique setting.

Really not much to be seen here, just an outdoor amphitheatre that's typically empty otherwise; the route here appears a bit difficult, seems like you're walking on private property inbetween buildings.
Awesome venue for concerts. It's best to bring your own seat cushion or pillow for comfort!

properties.name: Tvrđava Muzil
properties.description: Historic circular fortress on the Muzil peninsula in Pula, known for its preserved Austro-Hungarian military architecture, panoramic sea views, and prominent position above the southern coast.
reviews: I'm happy that this most preserved Pula's fort is now locked and secured, preventing it from robbing and vandalism. Thanks.

It was nice 'urban exploring' adventure, ruins everywhere, protected by army, but guardian is nice and army too! When sun goes down, be fast. Minutes matters. Some bushes (makija) can be spiky! Avoid them. Don't be barefoot.
Fort Muzil is closed to free entry from December 30, 2022. The facility is protected and maintained and can only be visited with prior notice or an organized tour.

properties.name: Utvrda San Michele
properties.description: Historic horseshoe-shaped fortress on the San Michele hill in Pula, built as part of the city’s defence system and known for its preserved military architecture and long later use within the hospital complex.
reviews: One of many fortresses located in Pula, and I would say one of my favorites, sadly no one maintains them and they are falling apart. This is beautiful city with rich history and so many potetial and 0 will to restore them... who knows, maybe one day the people who manage the city will get into their heads that Pula is not only the sea and the Arena... Pula is much more...
It could be an attraction if it wasn't completely neglected.

properties.name: Fort Grosso
properties.description: Historic hilltop fortress on Monte Grosso near Štinjan, known for its early military role in Pula’s defence system, preserved circular form, forest setting, and wide views towards the Brijuni area.
reviews: One of the 19th century artillery forts around Pula. Partly conserved, it is possible to enter and explore it and to climb the staircase to the platform. It is still astonishing despite being neglected in all ways.

Quite far from Pula. There are some nice places for mountain biking around here. To get to the fort you'll need to go off the main road at a point. There, Google Maps will get a little lost but just keep going on this dirt road. At a point you will find a sign with some info about the fort and there you are. Don't let the closed blue gate scare you, the entrance to the fort is to it's right. Quite the nice place. Awesome views.

A majestic view from here! I think it's the best view among all those local towers. It is a few steps from Stinjan, but worth visiting, as well as going further to the fort closer to the sea (Valmaggiore)

Nestled atop the city’s lush hill, the fortress offers a glimpse into Pula’s captivating past, intertwined with stories of power, conquest, and resilience. When I visited not long ago, the wear and tear were evident, and parts of the fortress have collapsed. While it poses certain risks, history enthusiasts may find the journey worthwhile. However, if you choose to visit, please proceed at your own risk and exercise caution, but those who go there will not regret it.

properties.name: ruševine Hidrobaze
properties.description: Former military ruin in the Hidrobaza area near Štinjan, currently in a neglected state and notable for its abandoned structure, graffiti, and position near the beach and promenade.
reviews: Interesting ruins (of an old seaplane base if I got it right) close to the beach. As usual in lost places close civilisation a lot of waste, racial slurs graffitied, and broken glass. Still quite interesting to see, but maybe don't bring your kids, don't go alone (floor partially collapsed), and watch your steps.
I don't know who owns it at the moment, but it's an important memento of Hungarian history: a seaplane base headquarters. It's a shame.
It's a shame these ruins have been left to decay. A wonderful 'lost place.'

properties.name: Fort Kaiser Franz
properties.description: Historic coastal fortress on the island of Sveti Andrija in Pula, known for its circular form, layered military history, and strategic position in the middle of the harbour.
reviews: Nice fort. It's a pity that it is neglected and completely overgrown. While sightseeing, it feels like walking through a jungle. There are a lot of snakes and rats. On the roof of the fortress there are a lot of hidden ventilation holes through which you can fall, so you need to be very careful.

properties.name: Dječje igralište Viška ulica
properties.description: Neighbourhood playground in Pula with basic play equipment, a fenced play area, and a family-friendly setting near Fort Casoni Vecchi and St. Paul’s Church.

properties.name: Lost Beach
properties.description: Hidden sandy beach near Štinjan, known for its quiet natural setting, turquoise water, and relaxed atmosphere tucked away in a sheltered bay.
reviews: If you're near Pula, you need to visit the Lost Beach. Had a weeks holiday in Stinjan/Pula and we visited The lost Beach twice! Pleasant walk down to the beach and bar, both visits it was quiet and plenty of space to sit and sun bath, water is clear and warm, view is amazing and the atmosphere is perfect. It exceeded all expectations! It was amazing. It really is a picture perfect Adriatic Cove. A must visit!
A definite quiet beach to visit, hardly anyone here. No facilities unfortunately but to be fair there was a bar being built when we arrived but yet to open the following weekend.

Gorgeous little bay with the most atmospheric beach bar. Came in both daytime and nighttime and neither disappointed. Vibes there are so relaxed, the chill out music and general feel of the place is definitely something you need to experience yourself

properties.name: Povijesni i pomorski muzej Istre
properties.description: Historic and maritime museum in Pula, housed in the 17th-century Venetian fortress on Kaštel, known for its collections on Istrian history, seafaring heritage, and commanding views over the city.
reviews: I appreciated the outstanding view but I think they should give you an audio guide as the museum ticket has a price but then looking inside you are just on your own hopping to understand correctly the long readings on the rooms.

Learning something new is amazing. Those tunnels were built long time ago even before my grandpa's time. Just to see that things are not always what seems to be, gives you time to reflect and appreciate what you have now. History is teaching us a good lesson let's don't make the same mistake again. If visit Pula this location gives you a 360 view over the city. The Colosseum is on the East side of the fort.

Pretty great museum if you like history and museums, there's a few rooms on the main level of the citadel. Otherwise, I'd say it's nothing special and the other levels and underground of the citadel are more fascinating.

properties.name: Plaža Jupiter
properties.description: Well-maintained designated beach on Veli Brijun, known for its stone-paved shore, two spacious terraces, and relaxing atmosphere. It is one of the official swimming areas on the island, appreciated for its cleanliness, safety, and beautiful sunset views.
reviews: Jupiter Beach on Brijuni Island in Croatia is a true paradise! The water is crystal clear and ideal for children to swim and play safely. For snorkeling enthusiasts, this beach is an absolute dream – you can observe countless fish in their natural habitat. A perfect place to enjoy the beauty of the underwater world. Highly recommended!
Cozy little beach

properties.name: Plaža Saluga
reviews: A beautiful beach with a shower, a bench and a small park. It has an entrance to the sea for people with disabilities.
A well-maintained beach for swimming, but not my type because it's concrete. There is shade and a bistro where you can eat and drink.

properties.name: Verudela Art Park
properties.description: Contemporary art park on the Verudela peninsula in Pula, featuring large sculptural installations, walking paths, and an open-air setting near Ambrela Beach designed for relaxation, play, and socialising.
reviews: Discovered by accident, the park has the bare bones of an excellent experience but is now in need of new and more exhibits. Location is great and good place to escape from the beach on a hot and busy day. Maybe time to partner up with similar organisations in other countries?

In Verudela, near Pula, there's a charming place called Verudela Art Park. It's a series of original installations set up in an empty lot between the beach, a parking lot, a restaurant, and sports fields. The meaning of these art objects isn't described anywhere, but they're surrounded by a rusty (purposefully) metal wall stenciled with the names of seas and oceans, hinting at their significance. The whole thing looks especially striking in the backlit evening light.
Beauty. Simplicity. Twist some metal ropes and it looks amazing.

properties.name: Tourist Info Centre
properties.description: Tourist information office in the heart of Pula on the Forum, offering maps, travel information, and visitor services from a historic building in the city’s main square.
reviews: Great, friendly service. Printed our boarding passes without charge when the EasyJet App did not work at the airport. Excellent
For the first time, just a phone conversation. The employee was extremely kind, warm. She answered all my questions without any hesitation, simply and clearly. Thank you very much.

Excellently trained and friendly team of employees always ready to help with information. A good team greatly contributes to the tourist offer of the city of Pula. Well done and keep up the good work.

properties.name: Fort Valmaggiore
properties.description: Historic Austro-Hungarian fortress in the Valmaggiore area near Štinjan, known for its robust military architecture, underground tunnel, and abandoned setting close to the coast.
reviews: Cool fort to visit. The fort has many rooms near the entrance, with two tunnels leading to a deeper bunker that has openings for shooting. Of course, some bats live inside, so please don’t disturb them. You can fully explore the fort in about 20 to 25 minutes. Some corridors are closed, and there’s no old equipment left inside the bunker. There is no parking nearby, the closest parking is a 10min hike. If you're into exploring or world war history I would recommend visiting.

One of the 19th century artillery forts around Pula. As others it was conserved with EU funds few years ago but it is obviously left to decay after that. Very interesting nevertheless. I suggest going here in a pair or a group as some of the spaces are obviously used by drug consumers.
Spooky place if you’re alone there, but still fun to investigate. Worth to visit if you in these parts. Parking is before bike sign, short walk from there.

properties.name: Mozaik - Kažnjavanje Dirke
properties.description: Archaeological site in central Pula featuring the Roman mosaic of the Punishment of Dirce, a rare and finely preserved mythological floor mosaic discovered near St. Mary Formosa.
reviews: Kind of hard to find. You have to go around the back of the main street and through a parking lot. It almost looks like you are walking into someone's driveway. It is not well lit so if you want better pictures, go during the day.
Very nice but looks like in a hidden area. You can follow the sign as well as use Google Maps. You may enter any time during the day at no charge.

Absolutely go and see it - a beautifully preserved mosaic from I believe the 2nd or 3rd century AD within it's own protective room. No entry fee or anything; just observe the mosaic from street level and it is about 3-4 feet below street level. A little bit off the beaten track and the street signs aren't clear, but regardless it's a quiet and nice area so definitely make the detour.

properties.name: Herkulova vrata
properties.description: Ancient Roman city gate in Pula, known as the city’s oldest preserved Roman architectural monument, with a carved head of Hercules and an inscription linked to the founding of the Roman colony.
reviews: Small but historic. The Hercules Gate is easy to miss, but worth a quick stop. Ancient Roman carvings still visible—great for history lovers!
Cool history, worth routing your walking path on your way to some of the more popular spots in Pula.
The historical plaque is on the inside of the gate, making it hard to find, but still very picturesque.

properties.name: Stara parna lokomotiva
properties.description: Historic steam locomotive displayed in Pula near the railway station, known as a preserved example of early 20th-century railway engineering and the city’s industrial heritage.
reviews: It can be seen next to the Pula train station, is easily accessible and can be photographed.

properties.name: MEMO Museum of good Memories
properties.description: Interactive multimedia museum in Pula near the Arena, dedicated to everyday life from the 1950s to the 1980s, with nostalgic objects, reconstructed interiors, and hands-on exhibits.
reviews: We adored this little museum as we could compare our childhood experience with one in a different country. Plenty to see and some exhibits could be handled. We could also take our dog, which was a definite bonus! Tickets were not expensive. Very enjoyable!

If you were born in 1970 - 1980 and you would like to remind, how it looked like in your home, just go and visit this museum. There is lot of stuff, which was used in those years and in fact, comparing to today, the life is much easier, but quicker. This museum is located on the way from Amfitheater to the city centrum, so you should not miss it with your mobile navigation. It is worth to visit.
Very interesting museum if you are nostalgic person. We spend half hour inside. You can try and touch things. You can feel life from the past.

properties.name: Zerostrasse
properties.description: Underground tunnel complex beneath central Pula, created as a wartime shelter system during the Austro-Hungarian period and today presented as a historic site known as Zerostrasse.
reviews: Nice tunnel system that is easy to navigate. There seems to be very little information about the tunnels themselves and more information about various things that happened in Pula around the area. Tickets to the tunnel gove you access to the fort above.

Tunnels underneath the citadel with access to the citadel included in the price if you work your way up. I did this in reverse and made my way down through the tunnels. Blissfully cool after the heat of the exterior. Fascinated by the tunnels and side rooms. Would have liked to see more information provided on their wartime use, and not just learn about the trams. Found a lovely piece of art painted onto a wall in one of the side rooms, left there by a previous resident who had tried to bring a little of the outside indoors perhaps? A little forgotten masterpiece just waiting to be discovered. Just kind of stumbled across it as I explored, but maybe should have been signposted with a bit of context provided. The tunnels exit at locations in the old town. As a tourist myself this left me almost instantly in the shopping area at the end of my visit. I am sure not the original builders intention, but very welcome for someone like myself trying to get the most out of their day.

Fascinating underground tunnels. Zerostrasse is a network of WWI-era tunnels beneath Pula, originally built as shelters. It’s a cool and quiet escape from the summer heat, with interesting historical displays along the way. Not a long visit, but definitely worth it if you're curious about the city's wartime past.

properties.name: James Joyce
