properties.description: Bronze statue of James Joyce in central Pula near the Arch of the Sergii, commemorating the famous Irish writer’s stay in the city and its place in his early literary life.
reviews: This statue made we search more about this Irish novelist. He was an important writer whose books are read today. Here you can take selfies with the statue and enjoy a cup of coffee in a table next to the statue.
Nice sculpture. Beside a restaurant. If not known, or checked carefully, one can miss it for a person waiting outside a restaurant.
Nice statue. You can sit with him.

properties.name: Omir Bed & Breakfast
properties.description: Bed and breakfast in central Pula, offering air-conditioned rooms, breakfast, free WiFi, and a convenient location within walking distance of the Arena and other main sights.
reviews: Very nice helpfull personel, they help us in car rent and give information of best restaurant in Pula. Nice clean room, good location. Sincerelly recommended.

Very good hotel. Convenient location - close to the center and not far from the sea. Delicious breakfasts, friendly staff, clean rooms. I was pleasantly pleased by the fact that when we left early, the owner himself offered and left breakfast for us at the reception. I highly recommend
Excellent location with incredibly nice and helpful owners! Perfect accommodation for a weekend in Pula.

properties.name: Spomenik streljanim mornarima
properties.description: Memorial sculpture in Valerijin park in Pula, dedicated to the sailor and associated with the remembrance of the 1918 sailors’ revolt in the Bay of Kotor.

properties.name: Grobljanska crkva sv. Antuna
properties.description: Ruins of the former cemetery church of St. Anthony on Brijuni, a Gothic church dating from the 14th–15th century, located outside the former medieval settlement. The site includes traces of old frescoes, a stone bier inside the church, and remains of the cemetery that was in use from the Middle Ages until the 18th century.
reviews: Old graveyard chapel that has been used for preparating the dead before the burials. Still a calm and enchanted place that should be visited with awe and respect.

Old church, nearly fallen apart - no roof, some walls left standing. Outside the former medieval settlement was the cemetery church of St. Anton. The former residents of Brijuni found their final resting place around the church.

properties.name: Vidikovac Javornik
properties.description: Restored historic viewpoint on Veliki Brijun, known for its Austro-Hungarian metal structure, peaceful forest setting, and panoramic views over the island, sea, and nearby islets.
reviews: Pretty great spot for a look out - multi layered observation tower in the middle of the forest, easy to climb up, and lots of space on top to view all around. There's several benches. It is a bit more overgrown with trees, so views somewhat obstructed. This place is a bit difficult to get to, no clear signs - there's only one main trail from the north, it's not paved; you do not have access from the side of the villas.
Forest covered most of the view but still very nice to visit as it has few benches on the top of the tower to relax and enjoy the view and the breeze.
Nice place, not much of a view since surrounding forest outgrew the structure - on the plus side, very quiet and peaceful place

properties.name: Sfinga Verudela
properties.description: Apartment accommodation in the Verudela area of Pula, offering a terrace, free parking, free WiFi, and a convenient location within walking distance of Saccorgiana Beach.
reviews: Very lovely family holiday stay.

Very beautiful place in a quiet part of town. Walking distance to the beach 5 min. Shopping center 5-7 min by car. The equipment of the large apartment is absolutely sufficient. The rooms and living room have their own balcony. The air conditioning unit cools all rooms sufficiently. The price corresponds to the quality and comfort. Great satisfaction.

properties.name: Villa Nicole
properties.description: Spacious wellness villa in Pula with a private pool, sauna, jacuzzi, garden, and family-friendly outdoor facilities, offering a peaceful stay a short drive from the city centre and nearby beaches.
reviews: We stayed there for a week and would go back in a heartbeat. The host was incredibly nice, everything was clean, air-conditioned, and new. Plenty of space for eight people. The beach and shops are just a stone's throw away. Simply a top-notch place!

Booking with Kristina was incredibly easy. All our questions beforehand were answered very quickly. Her husband, Sandi, showed us around the house upon arrival; he was also super friendly and easygoing. The kitchen was very well-equipped. They even delivered dishwasher tablets to our door. We felt very comfortable and can wholeheartedly recommend the house and the owners. While you do need a car to get to the beaches, restaurants, and shops, everything is easily accessible. Thank you so much for the wonderful stay!

properties.name: Mar Mar Apartments
properties.description: Apartment in Pula offering a fully equipped kitchen, free WiFi, free parking, and a convenient location close to Valkane Beach, the Arena, and other main city attractions.
reviews: The apartment for two people is quite welcoming, tidy, clean, and cared for down to the smallest details.
Cool apartment, great location, very nice landlords and excellent service

We are currently staying in the MarMar apartments. It is extremely clean and tidy.  We met the wife and she showed us around the apartment and were everything is.  Honestly no complaints at all - both her and Mario have been so friendly and helpful and considerate. The only thing I would add, though it doesnt affect the review or amount of stars, but from personal experience of owning rental units - I would provide papertowel and serviettes/napkins.  Something so minor. But so far, we are loving the apartment! Great experience and great comfort.  The bed is so extremely comfortable. 10/10 all around :)

properties.name: Hostel Enjoy
properties.description: ne radi(?)

properties.name: Istrian Olive Oil Museum
properties.description: Museum in central Pula near the Arena, dedicated to the history and culture of Istrian olive oil, with interactive exhibits, guided tastings, and a shop featuring award-winning local products.
reviews: Definitely recommend the tour including tasting. Museum is quite small but still you can learn a lot of interesting stuff about olive oil and its history. But the tasting was the most catchy part!

Lovely museum with very interesting information. Highly recommend the extended (gold) tasting, as you get to try a lot of different flavours. Not doing any tasting when you go here means that you are missing out. The people working there were very nice and spoke good English.

properties.name: Avion-vjetrokaz
properties.description: Outdoor aviation attraction near Pula at the Medulin sports airfield, featuring a former light aircraft mounted as a wind vane and memorial display celebrating local flying heritage.

properties.name: Četiri elementa
properties.description: Public sculpture on Valkane in Pula, known as Four Elements, set near the tennis courts and football field as a distinctive outdoor artwork in the park area.

properties.name: Spomenik Vladimiru Gortanu
properties.description: Memorial sculpture on Lungomare in Pula dedicated to Vladimir Gortan, an important symbol of Istrian anti-fascist resistance, placed near the site where he was executed in 1929.
reviews: Beautiful monument. What is sad, apart from the story about Gortan himself, is that it's a wild parking place with cars parked 20cm from the monument all around it.

properties.name: Titov Cadillac
properties.description: Historic Cadillac Eldorado Cabriolet on Brijuni, displayed as one of the park’s best-known attractions and associated with Josip Broz Tito, who used it to drive around the island with numerous distinguished guests.
reviews: Nice car in excellent condition like it’s new fully restored.
Excellent model with top-notch equipment for that time. Preserved and in excellent condition. Can be rented for driving

properties.name: kayak tours | pulsevents.hr
properties.description: Guided kayak adventure in Pula offering coastal exploration, sea caves, snorkeling, and scenic views of the city’s rocky shoreline, suitable for both beginners and experienced paddlers.
reviews: It was an amazing experience with plenty of challenging activities included: kayaking, snorkeling, cliff jumping, passing through canals within the rocks. The monitors are super friendly, speakgood English and take good care of the group. It is easily doable with kids at the age of 7 and 13 in a three place kayak. The monitors made plenty of underwater photos of all member of the group with good quality cameras. Water or beers were provided at the bay when we stopped, just like the swimming Google's for snorkeling. Highly recommended.

This was truly one of the best experiences of my life!! Jhonnes was an excellent guide who did everything he could to make it a great trip. He is also amazing at capturing picturesque moments. My family and friends that saw the pictures were in aww and asked who the photographer was. I would highly recommend this adventure to anyone considering it.

A fantastic experience from start to finish. Well organised and very patient with us as inexperienced kayakers. We were kept updated and when our taxi left us in the wrong place a quick phonecall to them and they sorted everything to get us there on time. First class service, we were with teenagers who loved it too. Thank you

properties.name: Spomenik radnicima brodogradilišta
properties.description: Memorial sculpture at the entrance to the Uljanik shipyard in Pula, dedicated to shipyard workers who died in the anti-fascist struggle during World War II, and known for its dynamic bronze figures and symbolic connection to the city’s industrial history.

properties.name: Baretini - Studio Lungomare
properties.description: Modern and bright studio apartment in Pula, ideal for two guests, with a south-facing balcony, sea view, free parking, and a great location just a short walk from the beach and nearby beach bars.

properties.name: Kayak Centar Pula
properties.description: Kayak tours in Pula offer an active way to explore the coastline through paddling, snorkeling, and swimming, with options including island, cliff and cave tours, sunset paddles, morning tours, and kayak rentals for solo visitors, couples, or families.
reviews: We booked this tour via an enquiry through the website but received communication via Whatsapp, which was very helpful. We were able to clarify stuff and gave us confidence to book. Our guide was great, he knew the area well having grown up locally and was friendly and happy to chat with all of the group, as well as imparting his local knowledge. The kayaking route was brilliant, not too strenuous with lots of great views and a break for swimming, snorkelling (snorkel & mask provided) or relaxing. The drinks and snacks provided by the guide was a great touch. The guide also took plenty of 'action' pictures and we all received a link to download the pictures for FOC, great keepsakes. Definitely recommend.

We had an amazing time on the 3-hour sea kayak excursion with Pula Kayak Centre! Toni, our guide, was fantastic super knowledgeable, friendly, and made the experience feel really personal. The scenery was absolutely stunning, with crystal-clear waters and beautiful coastal views. Toni took us to some hidden spots and made sure we were all comfortable and safe throughout. It was a perfect blend of adventure and relaxation. Highly recommend this tour to anyone looking for an unforgettable experience in Pula!
Super nice! Really worth! We paid 26 euro for a 2 person Kayak in 2 hours. It was perfect time to explore some islands around Verudela!

properties.name: Paula von Preradović
properties.description: Memorial plaque dedicated to Paula von Preradović, poet and author of the lyrics of the Austrian national anthem, marking the house near Pula’s Naval Park where she spent part of her childhood and youth. The plaque commemorates her literary legacy and her lasting connection to Pula.
reviews: Memorial plaque for the poet of the Austrian national anthem, who also wrote poems about Pula and lived here for some time.

properties.name: Žena s mijehom
properties.description: Žena s mijehom is a bronze sculpture by Frane Kršinić, created in 1956 and located in Brijuni National Park as part of the Živa voda memorial site. The sculpture depicts a woman bending forward and pouring water from a goatskin bag into a stone basin below, symbolizing the importance of fresh water on the island. It is both an artistic landmark and a memorial to the technical achievement of discovering and drawing underground spring water on Brijuni.

properties.name: Fazanerija
properties.description: Small animal and bird area where visitors can see peacocks, turkeys, guinea fowl, parrots, and other birds, while the site also serves as a recovery and temporary care center for wild animals.
reviews: Don’t miss seeing this bird! Easy to find, unique experience
Koji is awesome! He knows a few words, best one being 'tito'
The animals seem content, and the facility was well-maintained. Of course, the buildings are old, and it's not a large zoo. But it's definitely worth a visit.

properties.name: Robert Koch
properties.description: Stone memorial plaque dedicated to the scientist Robert Koch, honoring his role in the fight against malaria and the transformation of Brijuni into a healthy and habitable island landscape.

properties.name: McDonald's PlayPlace
properties.description: Small children’s play area next to a fast-food restaurant, designed for family visits and casual breaks, with enclosed play equipment and a slide for younger kids.

properties.name: Studio 7even Ground Floor
properties.description: Compact ground-floor studio apartment with a double bed, private bathroom, dining area, and equipped kitchen, suitable for a comfortable stay near beaches and the city center.
reviews: Quiet location, in a ground-floor apartment building; parking is not exclusive, but available and convenient; the owner was very kind and provided all the necessary information for our stay; the studio apartment was clean and well-equipped. Excellent value for money.
The house is very welcoming and has everything you need. They even left us some snacks and water. All the necessary appliances are there. There are no linen changes.
Small but well-maintained apartment. Just a few minutes' drive from the beaches and the city center.

properties.name: Spomenik Sonyju
properties.description: Life-size elephant memorial sculpture placed on a hill near the harbor, created in memory of a beloved elephant that lived on Brijuni for many years. The artwork is hand-carved and decorated with map motifs symbolizing the places connected to its life and journey.

properties.name: Tragovi dinosaura
properties.description: A remarkable geopaleontological attraction where preserved dinosaur footprints reveal that ancient reptiles once walked this area around 130 to 100 million years ago. More than 200 footprints have been discovered across Brijuni, with visitor-accessible tracks on Veliki Brijun offering a rare and valuable glimpse into the prehistoric past.
reviews: At first glance, nothing 'special' but when do you ever see real dinosaur footprints? I thought it was cool that they had a matching dinosaur statue next to it. If you're on the island, you should stop by there, too.
Traces 100 million years old. Super exciting.

It was simply amazing to see something like that. When and where else do you get the chance to admire original dinosaur footprints!? And the best part was, I was completely alone there (off-season, early June, around 5 pm), simply wonderful.

properties.name: Golub mira
properties.description: A symbolic outdoor sculpture created by Dušan Džamonja and donated to the Brijuni archipelago in memory of Josip Broz Tito. Made of corten and formed from welded iron chains, it was conceived as a powerful expression of peace and freedom, combining modern artistic form with historical remembrance.

properties.name: Vea Histria Apartments Pula
properties.description: Elegant and well-equipped apartments in a quieter part of Pula, designed for a comfortable and practical stay close to the city and nearby beaches. The units offer private terraces, modern kitchens, air conditioning, Wi-Fi and parking, making them a convenient choice for couples, families and guests who want easy access to both urban attractions and the coast.
reviews: Very good location, close to shops, restaurants, city center, port, Roman arena. It has its own parking which is a big plus for Croatia, where it is hard to find parking and everything is for a fee (price varies between 1.5-4 euros/hour). The beds are comfortable, apartment equipped with everything you need, it is spacious. Check-in is easy, you get the details from the owner. You are also close to the sea (which is rocky, you definitely need water shoes, otherwise you can't go).

The apartment was comfortable and clean, spacious enough, and not too hot despite the 35°C (95°F) outside. Convenient check-in and check-out, and car parking available. The location was also convenient, very close to the ring road, for us who traveled a lot in the surrounding area. If I were to return to Pula, this would be my first choice.
I recommend this place. The owner is always helpful. Quiet and peaceful.

properties.name: Apartment Sunčica
properties.description: A spacious and family-friendly apartment in a quiet residential area of Pula, ideal for a relaxed stay away from city noise. It offers a garden, terrace, private parking, Wi-Fi and a fully equipped kitchen, with added conveniences such as a children’s playground and easy access to the city, beaches and nearby nature areas.
reviews: Great and spacious apartment with loads of amenities.

We stayed for two weeks in this beautiful apartment with a garden and parking spaces, equipped with every comfort. The owners, who live upstairs and have separate access, were very quiet (like ghosts). The owner welcomed us with local delicacies and wine, a fruit basket... midweek for breakfast, she offered us a platter of local cured meats and cheeses, and as we appreciated it, she also gave us vacuum-packed cured meats they make, ice cream, and sweets for the children. Highly recommended; try it and believe it

The owner is very kind, and you can feel the love she has put into the apartment. The hygiene and amenities are of a very high standard, and the beds are comfortable. If we have the chance, we would love to return here.

properties.name: Rimski mauzolej
properties.description: Ancient remains of an octagonal Roman mausoleum dating from the 1st century, located near the Twin Gates, the Archaeological Museum of Istria and the old city walls. Only the lower part is preserved today, but it still reflects the architectural elegance and funerary traditions of Roman Pula, making it an interesting stop for visitors exploring the city’s archaeological heritage.
reviews: The mausoleum is actually a funerary monument, it should only be understood for this purpose. What is still visible is the base of the one used by the Romans, it being much taller, richly ornamented and placed outside the walls of the Roman city for reasons related to the legislation of the time regarding the burial of the dead. All the explanations, photographs and sketches help you understand and imagine what this construction was. Currently it is in a small park, across the road from the Gemini Gate

This octagonal wonder from the 1st century stands as a testament to Pula's rich Roman roots. It's right by a few buildings / cafe, and beside the park - aside from the stone structure itself, not much to see or do.

Everyone's running towards the double arch opposite... hmm... I found the octagonal mausoleum much better. Unfortunately, the explanatory plaque is missing. It's easily accessible. It's an excavation, and you're looking down at the structure from above!

properties.name: Marko Marulić
properties.description: A bronze public sculpture dedicated to one of the most important figures of Croatian literature, placed in a small park near the waterfront and the railway station. Its calm, dignified form reflects the intellectual and cultural legacy of the Renaissance writer, adding a literary and commemorative note to the surrounding urban landscape.

properties.name: Park Plaza Verudela
properties.description: A modern seaside resort on the Verudela peninsula offering well-equipped apartments, beautiful coastal views and easy access to the beach. Known for its seawater pools, family-friendly atmosphere and convenient location near Pula, it is a great choice for guests who want the flexibility of apartment accommodation with resort-style amenities.
reviews: All praise for a real little paradise in the embrace of the sea. The location is truly inspiring, beautiful and unusual. The staff, service and facilities are great. Kudos to the people who clean and maintain the rooms. Cleanliness is up to the task. The friendliness of the people at the reception is commendable.

This is our second time here. It's very quiet in the off-season, yet truly beautiful. The complex is large and offers plenty of variety. The apartments are well-equipped; we had two bathrooms and ample space. I can easily imagine coming back a third time.

Beautiful location surrounded by greenery and close to the sea, creating a relaxing atmosphere throughout the stay. The food was good with a nice variety, and the hotel is conveniently located just a short distance from Pula’s old town, making it easy to explore the historic sights while still enjoying a peaceful setting.

properties.name: Četvrt Sv. Teodora u Puli
properties.description: A historic quarter in the northeastern corner of Pula’s old town, known for its exceptionally rich archaeological layers that reveal nearly 3,000 years of urban history. Excavations uncovered remains from prehistoric times, Roman temples, a domus, baths, early Christian churches and one of the largest amphora finds in the Mediterranean, making this area one of the city’s most important archaeological zones.
reviews: A discovery of numerous remains from various cultural periods during the three-thousand-years long history of the city of Pula. They're pretty old and run down, not taller than a foot or two; only worth walking through if you're already in the area.

A true hidden gem in the heart of Pula. The San Teodoro neighborhood is a fascinating archaeological site, where you literally walk through centuries of history: from Roman ruins to the Middle Ages, passing by the Temple of Hercules and the ancient amphorae discovered there. It's not a huge area nor overly touristy, but it's perfect for those who love archaeology and want to discover something authentic and peaceful. I recommend a guided tour to fully appreciate it!

properties.name: Lino Mariani
properties.description: A bronze memorial bust dedicated to an antifascist partisan, trade unionist and worker remembered for his role in the turbulent post-war history of Pula. Placed near the ancient city walls and Hercules Gate, it adds a modern historical layer to this Roman setting and commemorates local resistance, labour struggle and civic memory.

properties.name: Sol Garden - Hostel with a Soul
properties.description: A simple and budget-friendly stay close to Pula Arena and the city’s historic center, offering dormitories and twin rooms with access to shared bathrooms. Guests can use a fully equipped shared kitchen, relax in the garden or on the terrace, and easily reach the waterfront, restaurants, bars and main transport connections on foot.
reviews: The hostel was very clean, it had a pleasant smell, and it was very quiet. It has a large kitchen and a spacious balcony. In the rooms, there are also small lockers that can be locked using the card provided by the reception to access the doors. Nearby, there are 2–3 markets, a coffee bar, and it is also close to the Arena as well as the bus station.

The hostel is very good, quiet, and has several places nearby such as markets, coffee bars, and other shops. It also has a nice balcony, a large kitchen, and spacious bathrooms. The rooms are clean, air-conditioned, and everything is in order.
Location and Staff! Excellent environment

properties.name: Vidikovac Opatija (ostaci)
properties.description: Permanently closed(?)

properties.name: Apartment Falu
properties.description: A comfortable apartment in Štinjan with two bedrooms, a private terrace and practical amenities for a relaxed stay near the coast. Guests can enjoy an outdoor pool, children’s playground, free parking and easy access to Hidrobaza Beach, while Pula and Brijuni are both within easy reach.
reviews: We had a wonderful stay at Apartment Falu for our family holiday to Istria. Martina and Rob were the most kind and hospitable hosts.. The apartment was well equipped with everything we needed and was very clean. The pool was our personal favourite and we made good use of it during our stay. I would definitely return to Apartment Falu and would recommend it to others. Thank you Martina and Rob

We were greeted very warmly with freshly baked pastries. The hosts are helpful but not intrusive. The apartment is beautifully, practically, and lovingly furnished; we had the pool completely to ourselves.

properties.name: Apartments Antons
properties.description: A centrally located adults-only stay in the heart of Pula, just a short walk from the Arena, Kastel and other major historic sights. It offers comfortable modern accommodation with air conditioning, a kitchenette, private bathroom and free WiFi, making it a practical choice for couples and city-break travellers.
reviews: Just the perfect place to be, wonderful apartment and parking place in the old town, and a great host!
Great location, very clean, fully stocked with anything you could need, very responsive and friendly hosts, lots of hooks and outlets which was appreciated. Highly recommend!

Wonderful apartment in the old city. Centrally located for a great selection of bars, restaurants and shopping. Well equipped with plenty of useful information provided. Amazing hosts, attentive and happy to help without being intrusive. On a three night stay they checked in with us to make sure we had everything - couldn’t recommend enough . Thank you so much for a lovely stay

properties.name: Mate Parlov
properties.description: A public sculpture dedicated to one of Pula’s most celebrated athletes and a legendary figure in Croatian boxing. Located on Giardini, it honors his lasting connection to the city and keeps the memory of his sporting legacy alive in the heart of Pula.
reviews: The appearance of the statue doesn't give away that it's a boxer. Too bad.
A nice gesture and thanks from the city of Pula and its citizens

properties.name: Ribarska Koliba Resort
properties.description: A stylish seaside resort in Pula known for its long hospitality tradition, elegant rooms and scenic setting near Marina Veruda and Marina Bunarina. It offers a quiet stay close to beaches, the aquarium and major city sights, with modern amenities such as free WiFi, parking and a pool area.
reviews: I've been here a few times, with friends and family and I've been very satisfied every time. The rooms are spacious, clean and well laid out. The breakfast is excellent with a lot of choice and a beautiful view of the marina. The staff is friendly and fantastic, to be commended for their flexibility regarding check-in and check-out and all the necessary services and information. Real value for money!

Lovely euro boutique hotel that exceeds expectations. Great close in views of the marina from the third floor. Rooms are great, functional and have all the amenities. Very nice outdoor deck. On site Michelin restaurant is a bonus. You will have to Uber bus or drive to the Pula old town. There is a fine sea walk and beaches nearby and a few luxury hotels with nice eating options. Recommended.

Incredible stay. We stayed for 5 nights as part of our honeymoon journey throughout Croatia. We were kindly upgraded to the 'Fuoco 1' apartment and it was superb, immaculately clean, spacious with fantastic views of the marina from the private balcony. The staff were so friendly and helpful when my wife required some medical attention for her infected insect bite they signposted us to the tourist hospital. We dined at the on-site restaurant twice because the quality of food was so good and was reasonably priced. Aperol spritz only 8€ as an example. Highly reccomended and we hope to visit again in future anniversaries.

properties.name: Modo
properties.description: A modern seaside hotel in Pula set between the bays of Stoja and Valovine, offering sea-view rooms with balconies, free WiFi, free parking and easy access to nearby beaches. Its quiet location and convenient connection to the city make it suitable for families, couples and business travellers.
reviews: Great little hotel in a great location. The staff is very professional and friendly. The premises are modernly decorated and clean. A rich selection of food for breakfast.

Cosy hotel, spacious room with balcony overlooking the sea. The manager on our arrival was friendly and explained what to visit in Pula and nearby. In addition he suggested (and we went there on the last day) to visit Motovun, castle and village where you eat dishes with truffles. The breakfast of the hotel is great, varied both savory and sweet. At night absolute silence, very relaxing.

The location was amazing ! We had a balcony that was perfect for watching the sunsets . We easily walked into town ( there also is a bus option ) the rooms were clean and comfortable . My only negative was the AC shut off randomly . The front desk said it turned off every 2 hours and needed to be reset . This was not a great for a cool night sleep without getting up a number of times to reset the AC . Breakfast was very good and it was lovely to eat out on the deck . Staff was friendly and helpful .

properties.name: Apartman Elis
reviews: Very quiet place, not far from the beach.
Lovely holiday flat. Near center, friendly host.

properties.name: Funcic Apartments
reviews: Magnificent bed and breakfast just a few kilometers from Pula. The property consists of three small apartments arranged in a horseshoe shape, with a shared pool in the center. The property also includes an American bar and a large grill. Ljuba, the owner, is very helpful and kind.
Incredibly beautiful apartment, Ljuba is very nice. It's worth coming here; it's in a very quiet location close to the sea, only about 1.5 km away.
Clean, comfortable, and practical accommodation. The pool is also lovely. The hostess speaks perfect Italian and is very helpful. We will definitely be back.

properties.name: Villa Elite
properties.description: Spacious modern villa in Pula near the sea, offering two private outdoor pools, multiple en-suite bedrooms, large terraces, and family-friendly outdoor amenities for a comfortable group stay.
reviews: Amazing Villa! Had the best vacation ever, totally recommend it! Everything is clean and the people who rent are super kind.
Fantastic villa and a very nice landlady. We had a wonderful holiday. Highly recommended!!
